meta.task: π powers in BST formulas substrate-volume reading

pi_exponents_catalog.description: m_p/m_e = C_2·π^(n_C)·m_e. π exponent = n_C BST primary.
m_μ/m_e formula has π^12 total in denominator. exponent 12 = 2·n_C+2 = 2(n_C+1)
Bergman normalization c_FK has π^(9/2) = π^((g+rank)/rank) in denominator
TBD: D_IV⁵ Bergman volume in terms of π
α has no explicit π in BST lowest order

m_p_over_m_e_pi_reading: π^(n_C) = π^5; C_2 numerical coefficient; D_IV⁵ real-10-ball volume
m_mu_over_m_e_pi_reading: π^(2·(n_C+1)) = π^12; rank-2 squared Bergman correction
hypothesis: All π exponents in BST formulas are BST primary integer/ratio combinations
